businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomy, robotics, energy, and custom chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin reached a two-year high of 20.1%. Key growth catalysts ramp from April 2026 with Cybercab production and Optimus Gen 3.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg (~23x) and auto sector avg (~16x). Still deeply overvalued despite continued decline

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 consensus EPS of $2.53. UBS cut Q1 delivery estimates citing demand slowdown

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 2025 reached a 2-year high of 20.1%, signaling margin recovery despite pricing pressure

keyMetrics.name: Revenue TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 full-year revenue. Slight YoY decline but energy segment driving growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation. $44B total cash supports massive capex for Terafab and solar

keyMetrics.name: Net Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Compressed margins due to price competition and expanded R&D investment

growthDrivers.title: $2.9B Chinese Solar Equipment Deal: 100GW US Solar Target
growthDrivers.description: Reuters exclusive (3/20): Tesla negotiating $2.9B in solar manufacturing equipment from Suzhou Maxwell Technologies and others. Targeting 100GW US solar production by 2028, with Texas delivery by fall. Massive energy vertical integration play.

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Contract
growthDrivers.description: Samsung confirmed at AGM that Tesla AI6 (HW6) chips will be produced at its Taylor, Texas fab using 2nm (SF2P) process. $16.5B deal through 2033, though AI6 MPW production is ~6 months delayed.

growthDrivers.title: Terafab Semiconductor Unit Public Launch March 21
growthDrivers.description: Tesla publicly launching its Terafab project tomorrow (3/21). Targeting trillions of custom chips annually for AI training, FSD inference, and Optimus robots.

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Starting April, Macrohard AI Platform
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab line designed for hundreds of units per week. Macrohard (Tesla-xAI JV) enters enterprise AI as a new revenue stream.

growthDrivers.title: China Sales +35% YoY, Trump Visit Reset in 5-6 Weeks
growthDrivers.description: China-made Tesla EVs hit 127,728 units in Jan-Feb, up 35%+ YoY. White House announced Trump China visit reset within 5-6 weeks.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader. 8.2B FSD miles, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration are core moats. Samsung AI6 foundry contract accelerates custom chip ecosystem.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles driven. Overwhelmingly more training data than any competitor.
In-house battery (4680), custom chips (Terafab + Samsung AI6), software (FSD), charging infra (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M in 2025). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min charging tech

recentNews.headline: Pentagon Deploys 3 Warships, Thousands of Marines to Middle East; Oil Surges
recentNews.significance: WSJ reports Pentagon sending additional naval assets to Middle East. Oil prices surging, S&P 500 on track for longest weekly losing streak in a year. Direct hit to growth stocks.

recentNews.headline: Reuters Exclusive: Tesla Negotiating $2.9B Solar Equipment Deal with Chinese Firms
recentNews.significance: Solar panel and cell manufacturing equipment from Suzhou Maxwell Technologies. Targeting 100GW US solar production by 2028. Texas delivery by fall.

recentNews.headline: NHTSA Upgrades FSD Probe to Engineering Analysis: 3.2M Vehicles, Potential Recall
recentNews.significance: NHTSA escalated FSD safety probe to engineering analysis over poor visibility concerns. 9 crashes (1 fatal). Mandatory recall possible.

recentNews.headline: Terafab Semiconductor Unit Announced, Public Launch Set for March 21
recentNews.significance: Tesla formalizing its custom chip manufacturing business. Public launch tomorrow. Aims for trillions of chips annually.

recentNews.headline: Fed Holds Rates at 3.5-3.75%, Markets Now Pricing Zero Cuts in 2026
recentNews.significance: Fed held rates steady. Markets now discounting any rate cuts in 2026. S&P and Nasdaq continue multi-day decline.

analystOpinions.notableComment: 41 analysts consensus $406.84 (Hold). BNP Paribas $280 (Underperform), BofA $460 (Buy), Wedbush $600 (Outperform). Upside widened to 9.6% as price dropped.

risks.title: Middle East Geopolitical Risk + Oil Price Surge
risks.description: Pentagon deploying 3 warships and thousands of Marines to Middle East. Oil surging, undermining EV transition narrative. High rates + high oil = worst combo for growth stocks.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Engineering Analysis: Potential Mandatory Recall
risks.description: 3.2M vehicles under upgraded probe. FSD safety concerns in limited visibility. 9 crashes (1 fatal). Hardware recall would devastate robotaxi strategy.

risks.title: Fed Holds + Markets Price Zero 2026 Cuts
risks.description: Market participants now fully discounting any 2026 rate cuts. Worst possible rate environment for a stock at 222x P/E.

risks.title: BYD Competition Accelerating
risks.description: BYD sold 2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M in 2025. 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, and 5-minute charging tech closing the gap.

risks.title: $2.9B Solar Equipment China Export Approval Uncertainty
risks.description: Some equipment requires Chinese commerce ministry export approval. US-China tensions could delay or block the deal.

risks.title: Valuation Burden at 222x P/E
risks.description: 10x premium over S&P 500 average. Multiple compression risk intensifying as rate cut hopes evaporate.

buyReasons.title: $2.9B Solar Investment: Energy Vertical Integration
buyReasons.rationale: 100GW US solar production target. Self-sufficient energy supply is critical as data center power demand explodes.

buyReasons.title: Terafab Public Launch March 21: Custom Chip Era Begins
buyReasons.rationale: Launching tomorrow. No other automaker can do this. Success would complete AI/FSD/Optimus vertical integration.

buyReasons.title: China +35%, Trump Visit Reset for Tariff Relief
buyReasons.rationale: China market rebounding. US-China summit reset in 5-6 weeks could ease Shanghai factory regulations.

buyReasons.title: RSI Entering Oversold Territory, Historical Bounce Point
buyReasons.rationale: RSI approaching 30. Historically strong bounce levels. Down 24% from highs.

overallOpinion: The Pentagon is sending 3 warships and Marines to the Middle East, pushing oil prices higher and driving the S&P 500 toward its longest weekly decline in a year. Tesla dropped to $371.04, now 24% below its high.
Tomorrow's Terafab public launch and the $2.9B solar equipment deal are real catalysts, but NHTSA's upgraded FSD probe and the market pricing zero 2026 rate cuts are keeping a lid on any recovery.

RSI at 30 (oversold) with price well below the 200-day SMA ($394). Catalysts are stacking up (Terafab, Cybercab April, solar), but geopolitical and rate headwinds are stronger. Wait for the FSD probe outcome and Q1 results before adding.

investmentVerdict.summary: Middle East escalation and oil surge are compounding the Fed headwind, pushing TSLA to $371. At 222x P/E with RSI 30 oversold, there's technical bounce potential, but NHTSA FSD uncertainty and geopolitical risk make chasing dangerous. Tomorrow's Terafab launch and April Cybercab could be catalysts, but wait for Q1 results and regulatory clarity.

witty.hook: Terafab tomorrow, but Pentagon stole the show today
witty.text:
$TSLA $371.04(-2.44%)

Tomorrow Tesla unveils its chip fab. Today the Pentagon deployed warships to the Middle East. The future and the present are in a tug of war, and the present is winning.

Solar $2.9B, chips $16.5B, Cybercab April. A new card every week but oil prices don't care. P/E 222x, RSI 30, 24% off highs.

Hold. Terafab might change things tomorrow. Or the market might not care. Target $406.84(+9.6%).

empathy.hook: Tesla holders, this 24% drop is painful but the story isn't over
empathy.text:
$TSLA $371.04(-2.44%)

$490 was just months ago. Now it's $371. Middle East escalation, oil prices, evaporating rate cuts. It hurts. It's supposed to.

But the company isn't stopping. Terafab launches tomorrow. $2.9B solar deal in progress. Cybercab starting in April. RSI 30 has historically been a bounce zone.

No need to rush. FSD results and Q1 earnings will tell us if the thesis holds. Hold. Target $406.84(+9.6%).
